title: The Shape of Discovery: Detecting Topological Precursors to Technological Breakthroughs in the USPTO Patent Citation Network
description: Applies persistent homology to the U.S. patent citation network (~8M patents, ~100M citations, 1976-2023) to test whether topological signatures precede technological breakthroughs. Computes Betti numbers and persistence entropy on CPC subclass co-citation distance matrices in sliding time windows, then tests 26 cataloged breakthroughs against matched null models via superposed epoch analysis. Key findings: (1) beta-1 (topological loops) shows no systematic precursor signal (p=0.48); (2) persistence entropy also shows no significant signal (p=0.14); (3) no test survives Holm-Bonferroni correction — a clean null result; (4) topological features carry cross-domain predictive signal comparable to simple features in Leave-One-Group-Out classification (LR AUC 0.80 vs 0.82). Conceptual design, analytical framework, and implementation by Claude (Opus 4.6, Anthropic) via claude.ai. Implementation by Claude Code. Facilitated by Christopher Ortiz.
notes: Conceived by Claude (Opus 4.6, Anthropic) via claude.ai. Implementation by Claude Code. This project represents a novel application of persistent homology to patent citation networks. The null result for beta-1 as a breakthrough precursor is a genuine scientific finding.
